purpose: Foundational substrate of the Crystal Cutter family. A generic 'perpetual reactionary growth' engine where any deformer slots in (Displacer in this demo) to drive self-feedback geometry evolution. Each frame: read previous state → apply deformer → voxelize → polygonize → store as new state.
visual_result: Sphere seed grows into beautiful organic blobby coral-like accumulation by f30. Each frame's displacement compounds on the previous frame's voxelized result.
input_geometry: Sphere (5160) wrapped in Init State Connect generator — only used as frame-0 seed.
spenser_canonical_interpretation: Quote: 'essentially just a memory function driving perpetual reactionary growth — using a displacer here so you could use really any deformer and it just continues to redeform the new solved state'. Confirms the Displacer is a SWAPPABLE DEFORMER SLOT — the architecture is universal across deformer types.

hosts.role: EXTERNALIZED MEMORY PRIMITIVE — 5 nodes total: memory@ + framework. Pure single-purpose state buffer.

om_classic_generators.name: State from previous Frame
om_classic_generators.role: OM reference to Store-for-next-Frame; reflects memory's previous output

om_classic_generators.name: Displacer
om_classic_generators.role: THE SWAPPABLE DEFORMER SLOT — any deformer plugs in here

am_exposure_pattern_note: Store-for-next-Frame has zero AM exposure. Slider tuning happens on the Displacer (or whichever deformer occupies the slot) and on the Volume Builder.

key_connections.purpose: PURE FEEDBACK WIRE — memory holds whatever Variable_1 was on its last output. The 5-node Nodes Mesh's only logic.
Host's input geometry feeds into Memory's Geometry slot.
OM-LEVEL FEEDBACK — Instance reflects whatever Store renders. Reading 'previous frame state' anywhere in the scene = create another Connect Instance pointing here.
Frame N's Solved State becomes Frame N+1's Memory contents — the perpetual loop.
Frame-0-only role — provides initial geometry before the loop starts feeding itself.

output_routing.via: Connect's polygon output → visible mesh in OM
output_routing.host_visibility_path: Solved State renders as classic mesh; Volume Mesher → Volume Builder → Connect Instance → Displacer chain produces it. Store-for-next-Frame is a hidden helper.
output_routing.notes: OM-level orchestration — different from graph-internal output routing of scenes 02-06.

interesting_types: 5-node Nodes Mesh as pure memory primitive container
Connect Instance.INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK as OM-level feedback reference
Frame-feedback choreographed via OM generators (no graph-internal memory@ wiring orchestration)

core_idea_nodes.name: Store for next Frame (Nodes Mesh)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: EXTERNALIZED MEMORY HOST — the smallest possible useful Scene Nodes host. Holds state between frames, has zero other logic. Reusable as a stock template for any frame-feedback OM scene.

core_idea_nodes.name: State from previous Frame (Connect Instance)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: OM-LEVEL FEEDBACK reference — INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK to the memory holder lets any part of the scene read 'the previous frame state' as if it were a regular OM object. No graph wires needed.

core_idea_nodes.name: Displacer (or any deformer in the slot)
core_idea_nodes.type: 1018685 (Displacer) — but THE PATTERN supports ANY deformer
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: SWAPPABLE DEFORMER SLOT — the entire architecture's character changes by replacing this. Bend=spiraling, Smoothing=erosion, Subdivide=recursive growth, Twist=helical, Wind=flowing, etc.

scaffolding_nodes: Init State Connect — frame-0 seed only, not in steady-state loop
what_is_clever: Memory primitive externalized into a tiny dedicated 5-node host. Separates state-holding from simulation logic — swap simulation, keep memory unchanged.
Connect Instance.INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK lets multiple consumers reference the same memory snapshot without graph wires.
Deformer slot pattern: ONE stack, infinite growth simulations. Same 5-line architecture covers crystal growth, erosion, recursive subdivision, twisting accumulation, etc.
OM-orchestrated feedback is more artist-friendly than graph-internal memory@ wiring — visual, swappable, no graph editing required.
5-node Nodes Mesh proves you can ship the smallest possible useful Scene Nodes container.

gotchas.issue: Tracing the wire from 'Solved State Connect' back into 'Store for next Frame's host input is non-obvious — there's no explicit parent/child relationship. The feedback flows via implicit pull-evaluation order at frame-step time.
gotchas.evidence: OM tree shows Init State / Solved State / Store-for-next-Frame as siblings. Their interaction is choreographed by the per-frame evaluation order, not a wire.
gotchas.workaround: Trust the canonical interpretation (Spenser's): 'memory function driving perpetual reactionary growth — Displacer is one example, swap for any deformer'. Don't worry about tracing every implicit wire if the behavior is clear.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Create 'Init State' Connect (1011010) generator. Inside it: a Sphere (or any seed mesh).
2. Create 'Store for next Frame' Nodes Mesh (180420600) at top level. Build a 5-node graph: memory@ + framework. Wire memory.Variable_1.out → memory.in@<hash> for self-feedback.
3. Create 'Solved State' Connect (1011010) generator at top level. Inside it:
b. Inside Volume Mesher: Volume Builder (1039859)
c. Inside Volume Builder: Connect Instance (5126) named 'State from previous Frame', set its INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK to 'Store for next Frame'
d. Inside the Connect Instance OR as child of Volume Builder: a Displacer (1018685) — OR any other deformer (Bend, Twist, Smoothing, Subdivide, FFD, Wind, etc.)

4. The implicit per-frame loop: Solved State → renders polygons → Store-for-next-Frame's memory holds those polygons → Connect Instance reflects them → next frame's Volume Builder reads those + Displacer mods → repeat.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: perpetual_reactionary_growth
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Generic frame-feedback growth engine where any deformer plugs into a designated slot to drive perpetual self-reactionary geometry evolution.
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.node_count: 5 OM objects + 1 minimal Nodes Mesh (5 graph nodes) ≈ 6 host elements total
nodes: Solved State (Connect) wrapping Volume Mesher → Volume Builder → Connect Instance → ★ DEFORMER ★
Store for next Frame (180420600 Nodes Mesh, 5-node memory@ container)
Connect Instance with INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK = Store for next Frame
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: ONE skeleton, INFINITE growth simulations. Drop in different deformers without touching the feedback substrate. Crystal growth, lava flow, erosion, recursive subdivision, spiral accumulation, curve-following growth, pulsing organic shapes — all from the same 6-element OM stack with different deformers occupying the slot.
secondary_recipes: R26_externalized_memory_buffer — 5-node Nodes Mesh as pure memory primitive container (smallest useful Scene Nodes host)
R27_om_feedback_via_instance — Connect Instance + INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK pattern for 'previous frame reference'
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: Stock 5-node Nodes Mesh template needed in cinema4d-mcp asset library
Recipe declaration of 'swappable deformer slot' so agents can propose alternatives

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: OM-orchestrated feedback is the artist-friendly cousin of graph-internal memory@ feedback. Recipe library needs BOTH styles.
5-node Nodes Mesh = pure memory primitive container. Worth shipping as a default asset / template.
Connect Instance.INSTANCEOBJECT_LINK is the per-frame state visibility primitive. Multiple consumers, single producer.
Deformer slot pattern (swappable_deformer_slot) is a recipe composition primitive — recipes can declare 'fill this slot with any deformer of category X'.
Same 5-line stack covers Crystal Cutter / lava / erosion / recursive growth / pulse / etc. Maximum leverage per recipe.
